Strict Adversarial Evaluation · EVAL lane (Magpie)

Checkpoint Rail v5 — Browser Verdict

Phone-width 390 px · live companion 192.168.2.13:8639 · driven by real CDP Input gestures (touch-emulated), screenshot-verified — not code inspection. Re-evaluated 2026-07-23 after the F3 + F5 fixes landed.

◔ RE-EVAL PENDING · commit held The 2026-07-23 re-eval passed F1–F5, but F3 is being redirected (extended-marker list — rows along the bar, no inner scroll) and a new permanent gate F6 (marker↔tag Y-alignment) is added. Full F1–F6 re-eval runs once Velvet / Fresco land the redirect + alignment fix.

Re-evaluation post-fix · 2026-07-23

Velvet / Fresco landed the two fixes; the served bundle was rebuilt. Re-driven at phone width via press-hold scrub on the rail. Note: the rebuild renamed the DOM — the reveal is now .cr-ckpt-list with .cr-ckpt-row children.

F3

Full range T1 → LIVE — scrollable-list build

Superseded · redirect
gesture: press-hold + scrub the rail · enumerate rows, measure scroll + here-marker · (this build is being redirected)
Redirected 2026-07-23: the user is replacing this inner-scroll list with an extended-marker list (rows anchored along the bar, no inner scroll). The evidence below documents the scrollable-list build that passed; F3 will be re-evaluated against the new design alongside F6.

The reveal is no longer a 3-row fan — it is a full scrollable list of 155–157 rows spanning T1 → T155 → LIVE (scrollHeight 5766 > client 608, scrollbar thumb visible). The window scrolls to follow the scrub, the here-marker tracks the scrub position (measured hereY 448 → 586 → 620), and the focused row is highlighted with a grey band that also reveals its FORK action — placed left of the index, not crowding the title.

Minor, non-blocking: the panel dismisses on an idle timeout rather than on ESC / tap-away, and on open it centers slightly above the committed checkpoint (committed row sits just below the fold). Neither affects the gate.
F5

Focused / list rows — solid opaque background

Pass · fixed
gesture: read computed background + visual occlusion across drag / idle / committed states

The list panel background is now rgb(28, 28, 31) — fully opaque (no alpha), and the focused row highlight is rgb(43, 40, 28), also opaque. The transcript behind the panel is completely occluded — no bleed-through in any state (drag, idle, committed). Only the thin left gutter outside the panel shows transcript, which is correct (the panel intentionally doesn't span the card's full width). Opacity is consistent across states — the ghosted collision from the pre-fix build is gone.

F1 · F2 · F4

No-regression spot-check

Pass

F4 — the "155 CP" count badge stays legible top-right in every state (visibility:visible, opacity:1). F2 — press-scrub pops the list, the here-marker jumps/tracks to the scrub position, and the row FORK action fires. F1 — the panel idle-collapses on its own (verified it closed unprompted after interaction stopped); the idle-vanish concept holds, now applied to the full panel.

Permanent gates added 2026-07-23

F6

Here-marker ↔ focused tag — horizontal alignment

Pending
measure: marker.getBoundingClientRect().y vs focused-tag.getBoundingClientRect().y · every state

The here-marker (diamond on the vertical bar) and the focused checkpoint tag / row must sit on the same horizontal line (same Y) in every state — single idle tag, extended-marker list, and at the top (T1) and bottom (LIVE) boundaries. PASS only if the two element Y-centers align within a few px, verified by measurement + screenshot. This has regressed before, so it is now a permanent load-bearing gate on every checkpoint-rail eval.

Redirect context: F3 no longer opens a separate inner-scroll list — the here-marker extends into the list along the bar (rows anchored to the bar). That makes F6 alignment the primary correctness check for the reveal.

Notes & caveats

Desktop width: the companion at :8639 is forced-mobile (body.cookrew-mobile) regardless of viewport width, and the true desktop surface is the Electron renderer (HMR) which this external CDP-Chrome harness cannot screenshot. F3 (full list) and F5 (opaque bg) are structural and apply to both surfaces, and the reverted defect was mobile-specific — so phone width is the load-bearing gate. Recommend a quick desktop eyeball by Conductor before final merge.

▸ Original evaluation (pre-fix) — F3 & F5 as they failed
F3

Full range T1→LIVE

was Fail

The track + here-marker mapped the full range, but the revealed list was only a 3-row fan window (up / focus / down), not a full T1→LIVE scrollable list.

F5

Focused row overlap

was Fail

The focused row was a translucent overlay with no opaque background — the identical transcript title bled through behind the bold marquee line (ghosted collision), opacity inconsistent between states.

method: CDP real Input events on a device-emulated Chrome (390 px, max-width:700 media, touch on) against the live mobile companion
evaluated by Magpie · EVAL lane · re-eval PASS · 2026-07-23
